elements.text: Hello Udit! How can I help you today?

text: <@U0BDPH2TK0U> introduce yourself

Hello! I'm Hermes, your AI agent. 

Think of me as a highly capable technical partner with a direct line to your system. Unlike a standard chatbot, I don't just describe what I can do—I actually do it. I have a full suite of tools at my disposal, including a terminal, a web browser, and the ability to read, write, and execute code directly on your machine.

*Here is a quick overview of how I can help you:*

*   *Software Engineering:* I can explore your codebase, debug complex issues, write features, and manage the entire GitHub PR lifecycle.
*   *Research & Synthesis:* I can search the web, extract data from pages, and summarize academic papers or technical documentation.
*   *Automation:* I can set up recurring cron jobs to monitor systems, fetch data, or send you reports while you're away.
*   *Creative Work:* I can generate images, create architecture diagrams, and even help with songwriting or video animations.
*   *Memory & Skills:* I remember your preferences and project conventions across sessions. If I discover a complex workflow that works, I can save it as a "Skill" to reuse it instantly in the future.

Whether you need a quick script, a deep-dive technical investigation, or a complex project built from the ground up, I'm here to handle the heavy lifting. 

What are we working on today?

text: <@U0BDHPXCJP8> has joined the channel

Forge2 Sprint 1 - Hermes Orchestrator Prompt
You are the Product Owner and Orchestrator for the Forge2 Hackathon.
The project to build is *PulseDesk*, a multi-tenant customer support SaaS built using the following stack:
• Laravel 11 REST API
• MySQL
• Laravel Sanctum Authentication
• React 19 + Vite + Tailwind
• GitHub Actions CI/CD
Your responsibilities are:
1. Read the complete project requirements.
2. Create a high-level development roadmap.
3. Break the entire project into *exactly 5 small, independent sprints*.
4. Each sprint should contain:
Goal
User stories
Technical tasks
Expected deliverables
Acceptance criteria
5. Save Sprint 1 as `sprints/sprint-01.md`.
6. Post the Sprint 1 summary in <#C0BDDDW2YJX|sprint-main>.
7. After Sprint 1 planning is complete, assign ONLY Sprint 1 to <@U0BDHPXCJP8> in *<#C0BDPDJ5Y5A|agent-coder>*.
8. Wait for OpenClaw's completion report before assigning any additional work.
9. Do not write code yourself.
10. Do not skip human approval.
11. Keep each sprint focused enough to be completed in a single pull request.
The five sprints should roughly follow this structure (you may refine them):
Sprint 1
• Project setup
• Laravel backend
• React frontend
• Sanctum
• MySQL
• Initial CI
• README
• Folder structure
Sprint 2
• Organizations (multi-tenancy)
• Users
• Roles
• Authentication
• Tenant isolation
Sprint 3
• Ticket CRUD
• Search
• Filters
• Assignment
• Tags
Sprint 4
• Ticket conversations
• Internal notes
• Activity log
• Dashboard
• Metrics
Sprint 5
• SLA policies
• Notifications
• Testing
• Documentation
• Final polishing
When Sprint 1 is assigned, post the assignment in *<#C0BDPDJ5Y5A|agent-coder>* by tagging <@U0BDHPXCJP8>.
The assignment message should include:
• Sprint Goal
• Scope
• Files expected to change
• Acceptance Criteria
• Reminder to run tests
• Reminder to create a Pull Request after completion
After assigning Sprint 1, wait for OpenClaw to complete the work before proceeding.

text:
:clipboard: todo: "planning 5 task(s)"
:writing_hand: write_file: "sprints/sprint-01.md"

*PulseDesk Development Roadmap*

PulseDesk is a multi-tenant customer support SaaS designed for scalability and isolation. The development will follow a structured 5-sprint approach to ensure stability, testability, and incremental delivery.

*High-Level Strategy*
- *Backend:* Laravel 11 REST API with Sanctum for token-based auth.
- *Frontend:* React 19 (Vite) with Tailwind CSS for a modern, responsive agent interface.
- *Database:* MySQL with a multi-tenancy architecture (Organization-based isolation).
- *CI/CD:* GitHub Actions for automated testing and deployment.

---

*Sprint Breakdown*

*Sprint 1: Foundation & Infrastructure*
*Goal:* Establish the baseline project structure, connectivity, and CI pipeline.
- *User Stories:* As a developer, I want a working boilerplate of both frontend and backend so that the team can begin feature development.
- *Technical Tasks:* 
    - Initialize Laravel 11 project and configure MySQL.
    - Setup React 19 + Vite + Tailwind CSS.
    - Configure Laravel Sanctum for API authentication.
    - Create initial GitHub Actions workflow (Lint/Test).
    - Establish folder structure and `README.md`.
- *Deliverables:* Base repository, connected API-Frontend, CI pipeline.
- *Acceptance Criteria:* API responds to health check; React app renders; Sanctum is configured; CI passes on push.

*Sprint 2: Identity & Multi-Tenancy*
*Goal:* Implement the organizational hierarchy and secure tenant isolation.
- *User Stories:* As an admin, I want to create an organization and manage users within it so that data is kept separate between customers.
- *Technical Tasks:*
    - Implement `Organization` and `User` models/migrations.
    - Create Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) (Admin, Agent, User).
    - Implement Global Scopes in Laravel for tenant isolation.
    - Build Auth flows (Login, Register, Password Reset).
- *Deliverables:* Org/User management APIs, Auth screens, Tenant-isolated queries.
- *Acceptance Criteria:* Users cannot access data from other organizations; RBAC restricts API endpoints.

*Sprint 3: Core Ticketing Engine*
*Goal:* Build the primary CRUD functionality for support tickets.
- *User Stories:* As a customer, I want to create a ticket; as an agent, I want to search, filter, and assign tickets.
- *Technical Tasks:*
    - Develop Ticket CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete).
    - Implement advanced search and filtering (Status, Priority, Assignee).
    - Build Ticket Assignment logic.
    - Add tagging system for tickets.
- *Deliverables:* Ticket management dashboard, API endpoints for ticket lifecycle.
- *Acceptance Criteria:* Tickets are correctly associated with tenants; search returns accurate results.

*Sprint 4: Communication & Insights*
*Goal:* Enable rich conversations and performance tracking.
- *User Stories:* As an agent, I want to reply to tickets and add internal notes; as a manager, I want to see a dashboard of key metrics.
- *Technical Tasks:*
    - Implement Ticket Conversations (Threaded messages).
    - Build "Internal Notes" feature (invisible to customers).
    - Create an Activity Log for ticket auditing.
    - Develop a Dashboard with metrics (Avg resolution time, Ticket volume).
- *Deliverables:* Conversation UI, Internal notes system, Analytics Dashboard.
- *Acceptance Criteria:* Conversations are chronological; internal notes are hidden from end-users.

*Sprint 5: Quality, SLAs & Final Polish*
*Goal:* Ensure enterprise readiness, reliability, and professional documentation.
- *User Stories:* As a business owner, I want SLA policies to track response times; as a user, I want notifications for ticket updates.
- *Technical Tasks:*
    - Implement SLA Policy engine (Target response/resolution times).
    - Setup Notification system (Email/In-app).
    - Full E2E and Unit test suite expansion.
    - Final UI/UX polishing and documentation.
- *Deliverables:* SLA Tracking, Notification system, Final User Manual.

*Goal:* Establish the baseline project structure, connectivity, and CI pipeline to enable all subsequent feature development.

*Scope:*
- *Backend:* Laravel 11 REST API shell + MySQL configuration.
- *Frontend:* React 19 (Vite) + Tailwind CSS base.
- *Auth:* Laravel Sanctum setup for token-based API access.
- *CI/CD:* Initial GitHub Actions pipeline for linting and testing.
- *Documentation:* Project folder structure and comprehensive `README.md`.

- Green CI pipeline on push.

*Sprint 2: Identity & Multi-Tenancy* is now defined and ready for execution. :rocket:

I have saved the detailed specification to `sprints/sprint-02.md`.

*Sprint 2 Summary:*
*Goal:* Implement the organizational hierarchy and secure tenant isolation to ensure data is strictly separated between different customer organizations.

*Scope:*
- *Backend:* `Organization` and `User` models, Global Tenant Scopes for isolation, and RBAC (Admin, Agent, User).
- *Auth:* Full API suite for Registration (Org + Admin), Login (Sanctum), and User Management.
- *Frontend:* Registration and Login flows, `AuthGuard` for route protection, and Organization/Team management dashboards.

*Key Deliverables:*
- Tenant-isolated database schema.
- Full Authentication and RBAC system.
- Org/Team management UI.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
- :white_check_mark: *Strict Isolation:* Zero cross-tenant data leakage.
- :white_check_mark: *RBAC Enforcement:* API endpoints correctly restrict actions by role.
- :white_check_mark: *End-to-End Auth:* Seamless register $\rightarrow$ login $\rightarrow$ dashboard flow.

---

*Assignment: Sprint 2* :hammer_and_wrench:
CC: <@U0BDHPXCJP8> in <#C0BDPDJ5Y5A|agent-coder>

*Sprint Goal:* Identity & Multi-Tenancy
*Scope:* 
- Implement multi-tenant architecture (Organization-based isolation).
- Build RBAC and Sanctum-based Auth flows.
- Develop Org/Team management UI.

*Expected Files to Change/Create:*
- `database/migrations/..._create_organizations_table.php`, `database/migrations/..._add_org_id_to_users_table.php`
- `app/Models/Organization.php`, `app/Models/User.php`, `app/Scopes/TenantScope.php`
- `app/Http/Controllers/AuthController.php`, `app/Http/Controllers/OrganizationController.php`, `app/Http/Controllers/UserController.php`
- `src/pages/Login.jsx`, `src/pages/Register.jsx`, `src/pages/Settings.jsx`, `src/pages/Team.jsx`

*Assignment: Sprint 2* :hammer_and_wrench:
CC: <@U0BDHPXCJP8> in <#C0BDPDJ5Y5A|agent-coder>

*Sprint Goal:* Identity & Multi-Tenancy
*Scope:*
- *Backend:* Implement organizational hierarchy and secure tenant isolation.
- *Auth:* Full Authentication API suite (Register, Login, Logout, /me).
- *RBAC:* Role-Based Access Control (Admin, Agent, User) via Laravel Gates/Policies.
- *Frontend:* Auth flows (Login/Register), `AuthGuard` for private routes, and Org/Team management dashboards.

*Expected Files to Change/Create:*
- *Migrations:* `organizations` table, updated `users` table.
- *Backend:* `TenantScope.php`, `TenantManager.php`, `AuthController.php`, `OrganizationController.php`, `UserController.php`.
- *Frontend:* `Registration.jsx`, `Login.jsx`, `AuthGuard.jsx`, `Settings.jsx`, `Team.jsx`, `AuthContext.jsx`.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
- [ ] *Isolation:* A user from Org A cannot access any record belonging to Org B, even with a direct ID.
- [ ] *RBAC:* 'Agent' roles are restricted from admin actions (verified via 403 responses).
- [ ] *Auth:* Complete flow from registration (Org + User creation) to session persistence.
- [ ] *UI:* Functional Team and Settings pages for organization management.
- [ ] *Consistency:* All tenant-specific API responses are correctly scoped to the user's organization.

*Reminders:*
- :test_tube: *Run all tests*, specifically verifying tenant isolation and RBAC boundaries.
- :herb: *Create a Pull Request* upon completion for review.

:writing_hand: write_file: "sprints/sprint-03.md"

*Sprint 3: Core Ticketing Engine* :rocket:

I have generated the detailed instructions for Sprint 3. The full specification has been saved to `sprints/sprint-03.md`.

*Sprint 3 Summary*
*Goal:* Build the primary CRUD functionality for support tickets, including search, filtering, and assignment, ensuring all data is strictly isolated by organization.

*Scope:*
- *Backend:* Full Ticket CRUD API, advanced search/filtering (status, priority, assignee), and a tagging system.
- *Frontend:* Ticket listing dashboard, creation forms, and a detailed ticket view with assignment controls.
- *Security:* Strict enforcement of `Organization` global scopes for all ticket-related queries.

*Key Deliverables:*
- A functional ticket management system from API to UI.
- Verified tenant isolation (no cross-org ticket leakage).
- Tagging and assignment logic.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
- Tickets are only visible to members of the same organization.
- Agents can assign tickets to other agents within their org.
- Search and filters accurately narrow down the ticket list.
- Tags are persisted and displayed correctly.
- All code is tested and the CI pipeline remains green.

---

*Assignment: Sprint 3* :hammer_and_wrench:
CC: <@U0BDHPXCJP8> in <#C0BDPDJ5Y5A|agent-coder>

*Sprint Goal:* Core Ticketing Engine
*Scope:* 
- Implement Ticket CRUD and Advanced Search/Filters.
- Build Ticket Management UI in React.
- Implement Ticket Tagging and Assignment logic.
- Ensure strict tenant isolation via Laravel global scopes.

*Expected Files to Change/Create:*
- *Backend:* `app/Models/Ticket.php`, `app/Models/Tag.php`, `app/Http/Controllers/TicketController.php`, `database/migrations/...create_tickets_table.php`, `database/migrations/...create_tags_table.php`.
- *Frontend:* `src/pages/TicketsList.jsx`, `src/pages/TicketDetail.jsx`, `src/components/TicketForm.jsx`, `src/api/tickets.js`.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
- [ ] Tickets are strictly isolated by `organization_id`.
- [ ] CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) are fully functional.
- [ ] Search and Filters return the expected result sets.
- [ ] Agents can assign tickets to other agents within the same tenant.
- [ ] Tags are successfully added and retrieved.
- [ ] CI pipeline is green and unit/feature tests pass.

*Reminders:*
- :test_tube: *Run all tests* before submitting.
- :herb: *Create a Pull Request* upon completion for review.

_I have noted your request regarding *OpenClaw*; I will continue to monitor for their completion reports and trigger the next set of instructions automatically moving forward._

text:
*:rocket: Sprint 4: Communication & Insights*

*Goal:* Enable rich conversations, internal collaboration, and performance tracking to transform the ticketing system into a professional support hub.

---

*:memo: User Stories*
- *As an agent*, I want to reply to tickets and maintain a threaded conversation with the customer so that issues are resolved through clear communication.
- *As an agent*, I want to add internal notes to a ticket that are invisible to the customer so that I can collaborate with teammates on a solution.
- *As a manager*, I want to see a dashboard of key performance metrics (e.g., average resolution time, ticket volume) to monitor team efficiency.
- *As an auditor*, I want to see a complete activity log for every ticket to track all changes and interactions.

*:hammer_and_wrench: Technical Tasks*
*Backend (Laravel 11)*
- [ ] Implement *Ticket Conversations*: Create a `Message` model and API endpoints for sending and retrieving threaded messages associated with a ticket.
- [ ] Build *Internal Notes*: Add a flag to messages to distinguish between "Public" (Customer-facing) and "Internal" (Agent-only) notes.
- [ ] Create an *Activity Log*: Implement a system to log ticket events (e.g., status changes, assignee updates, priority shifts).
- [ ] Develop *Analytics Endpoints*: Create API logic to calculate aggregate metrics (Avg Resolution Time, Tickets per Day, Volume by Status).

*Frontend (React 19)*
- [ ] Build the *Conversation UI*: A chronological thread view for tickets with distinct styling for customer messages and internal notes.
- [ ] Implement the *Internal Notes Toggle*: A way for agents to switch between writing a public reply and a private internal note.
- [ ] Create the *Activity Feed*: A sidebar or tab showing the chronological history of ticket metadata changes.
- [ ] Develop the *Insights Dashboard*: A visual dashboard using charts/cards to display the key performance metrics.

*:package: Expected Deliverables*
- *Conversation System*: Fully functional threaded messaging and internal note-taking.
- *Audit Trail*: A detailed activity log for every ticket.
- *Analytics Dashboard*: A management view with real-time support metrics.

*:white_check_mark: Acceptance Criteria*
- [ ] Conversations are displayed in strict chronological order.
- [ ] *Security Check*: Internal notes must be strictly hidden from the customer's view via API authorization.
- [ ] The Activity Log accurately reflects every single change made to a ticket's properties.
- [ ] Dashboard metrics update accurately based on real ticket data.

text:
Sprint 4 plan is ready. Here's the summary:

_Sprint 4 — Conversations, Activity Log & Dashboard_

_Goal:_ Add threaded conversations + internal notes to tickets, build an audit activity log, and deliver a metrics dashboard.

_Scope:_
• *Ticket conversations* — public replies + internal notes, chronological thread, reply composer with type toggle
• *Internal notes* — visually distinct, role-gated (agents/admins only, invisible to customers)
• *Activity log* — auto-records ticket events (created, status change, assignment, priority change, message posted), timeline UI
• *Dashboard* — metric cards (open/pending/resolved/avg resolution time), charts (tickets by priority, by tag), per-agent performance table, date range + agent filters, 30s polling

_Files expected to change:_
• `backend/` — new migrations (ticket_messages, activity_logs), models, controllers (TicketMessageController, ActivityLogController, DashboardController), routes, tests
• `frontend/` — ticket conversation view, activity log panel, dashboard page with charts, new charting dep (recharts)

_Acceptance criteria highlights:_
• Public + internal messages work with role-based visibility
• Activity log captures all key ticket events
• Dashboard metrics endpoint returns correct aggregates
• Date range and agent filters functional
• All tests pass, CI green
• Single PR on `feature/sprint-04-conversations-dashboard`

Full plan saved to `sprints/sprint-04.md`.

Ready to assign to `<@U0BDHPXCJP8>` — say the word and I'll post it in #sprint-assignments.

text: :mag: session_search: "recall: "sprint 5 instructions""

Here are the instructions for the final sprint of the *PulseDesk* project:

*Sprint 5: Quality, SLAs & Final Polish :rocket:*

*Goal:* Ensure enterprise readiness, reliability, and professional documentation.

*:book: User Stories*
- *As a business owner*, I want SLA policies to track response and resolution times so that I can maintain service quality.
- *As a user*, I want notifications for ticket updates so that I am immediately aware of progress.
- *As a developer*, I want a comprehensive test suite and documentation so that the system is maintainable and stable.

*:hammer_and_wrench: Technical Tasks*
- *SLA Policy Engine:* Implement logic to track target response and resolution times and flag breaches.
- *Notification System:* Setup email and in-app notifications that trigger on ticket status or assignment changes.
- *Testing Expansion:* Develop a full suite of E2E and Unit tests to ensure core workflows are bug-free.
- *Final UI/UX Polishing:* Review and refine the interface for a professional, seamless user experience.
- *Documentation:* Complete the final User Manual and technical documentation.

*:package: Expected Deliverables*
- *SLA Tracking System:* Fully operational policy engine.
- *Notification System:* Functional multi-channel alerts.
- *Comprehensive Test Suite:* High-coverage automated tests.
- *Final User Manual:* Professional guide for end-users and admins.

*:white_check_mark: Acceptance Criteria*
- [ ] *SLA Breaches:* Any ticket exceeding the defined SLA time is clearly flagged in the UI.
- [ ] *Notifications:* Alerts are successfully triggered and delivered upon status changes.
- [ ] *Test Coverage:* At least 80% code coverage across critical backend and frontend paths.
- [ ] *UX Quality:* No major UI glitches; consistent styling across all pages.
- [ ] *Documentation:* A new user can navigate the system using only the provided User Manual.
